NOT a git repo at the root.\n- Contains one plugin namespace `omo` at `plugins/omo/` with 5 isolated components (each is its own vendored git repo - clones of `code-yeongyu/codex-{rules,comment-checker,lsp,ultrawork,ultragoal}`):\n  - **rules** (TS, 4793 LOC): `SessionStart`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`PostToolUse`/`PostCompact` hooks injecting `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`/`.omo/rules/**`. Runtime no-deps.\n  - **comment-checker** (TS, 1228 LOC): `PostToolUse` hook runs `@code-yeongyu/comment-checker` binary after `apply_patch`/`edit`/etc.\n  - **lsp** (TS, 5208 LOC + Python 2): Codex hooks + nested `packages/lsp-tools-mcp/` MCP server (SAME submodule URL as omodex/packages/lsp-tools-mcp: `https://github.com/code-yeongyu/lsp-tools-mcp`).\n  - **ultrawork** (Python only, 331 LOC): `ultrawork-detector.py` injects 5,821-char directive on `\\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\\b` match. `sync-agents.py` copies bundled `agents/*.toml` into `CODEX_HOME/agents`.\n  - **ultragoal** (TS, 5839 LOC): Durable multi-goal orchestration with `.omo/ultragoal/` state, steering directives via `UserPromptSubmit`.\n- Component sizes: each ~111M with node_modules (109M) + dist (~200KB) + .git (~2MB). Source only: ~1-5MB total.\n- Aggregate manifest at `plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` declaring `skills: ./skills/, hooks: ./hooks/hooks.json, mcpServers: ./.mcp.json`.\n- Aggregate `hooks/hooks.json` dispatches to component CLIs via `node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/<name>/dist/cli.js\" hook <event>` or `python3 \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/ultrawork/hooks/*.py\"`.\n- Aggregate `.mcp.json` registers `lsp` stdio server at `./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js`.\n- `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` declares marketplace `code-yeongyu-codex-plugins` with one plugin `omo`.\n- `scripts/install-local.mjs` is the existing Node installer:\n  - Reads marketplace.json\n  - For each plugin: `npm install && npm run build` in source\n  - Copies to `~/.codex/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>/<version>/` (filter excludes .git/node_modules)\n  - Runs `npm install --omit=dev` in cache\n  - Rewrites `.mcp.json` paths to absolute\n  - Writes `~/.codex/config.toml` blocks: `[features] plugins=true plugin_hooks=true`, `[marketplaces.<name>]`, `[plugins.\"<plugin>@<marketplace>\"]`, `[hooks.state.\"<key>\"]` with SHA-256 trusted_hash\n  - Links package.json `bin` entries to `~/.local/bin/`\n- Components are npm-based (npm@11.12.1), TypeScript via `tsc -p tsconfig.build.json`, vitest tests. Aggregate root has `npm workspaces`.\n\n### B. omodex target repo (`/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/`)\n- Bun-only runtime. ESM. Zod v4 config schemas. Commander.js CLI.\n- Plugin entry: `src/index.ts` → `src/testing/create-plugin-module.ts` → 7-step init.\n- CLI entry: `src/cli/index.ts` → Commander program in `src/cli/cli-program.ts` with 8 commands (install/run/doctor/get-local-version/version/refresh-model-capabilities/mcp-oauth/boulder).\n- bin entries (`package.json` lines 20-23): `\"oh-my-opencode\": \"bin/oh-my-opencode.js\"`, `\"oh-my-openagent\": \"bin/oh-my-opencode.js\"`. Both point to same JS shim.\n- `bin/oh-my-opencode.js` is a Node shim that detects platform/arch/libc/AVX2, resolves `oh-my-opencode-<platform>` package, spawnSyncs binary with `process.argv.slice(2)` (passes ALL args through).\n- 11 compiled platform binaries via `bun build --compile` from `src/cli/index.ts`. Output: `packages/oh-my-opencode-<platform>/bin/oh-my-opencode`.\n- Dual npm publish: `oh-my-opencode` + `oh-my-openagent` (rename package.json via jq in `.github/workflows/publish.yml`).\n- workspace packages (root package.json lines 8-19): rules-engine, ast-grep-core, ast-grep-mcp, utils, model-core, prompts-core, comment-checker-core, hashline-core, boulder-state, agents-md-core.\n- `packages/lsp-tools-mcp` is a git SUBMODULE pointing to SAME repo as codex-lsp's nested lsp-tools-mcp.\n- Convention for Core packages: `@oh-my-opencode/<name>`, version `0.1.0`, `type: \"module\"`, `private: true`, exports `./src/index.ts` directly (no build for core), `bun-types`, `tsgo --noEmit`.\n- Convention for MCP packages (ast-grep-mcp): has `build` script using `bun build src/cli.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm`, has `bin` entry, root `package.json files` includes its `dist/`.\n\n### C. CLI installer architecture\n- `src/cli/install.ts` routes to TUI or non-TUI installer based on `args.tui`.\n- `src/cli/cli-installer.ts` (150 LOC): validation → OpenCode check → addPluginToOpenCodeConfig → writeOmoConfig.\n- `src/cli/tui-installer.ts`: interactive @clack/prompts flow.\n- `src/cli/tui-install-prompts.ts`: subscription prompts per provider.\n- `src/cli/install-validators.ts`: validateNonTuiArgs/argsToConfig/detectedToInitialValues.\n- `src/cli/types.ts`: InstallArgs (claude/openai/gemini/copilot/opencode-zen/zai-coding-plan/kimi-for-coding/opencode-go/vercel-ai-gateway/skip-auth), InstallConfig, DetectedConfig.\n- `src/cli/config-manager/`: 28 files for OpenCode config manipulation. Key: `add-plugin-to-opencode-config.ts` writes `oh-my-openagent@<version>` to `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json(c)` plugin array. `write-omo-config.ts` writes `~/.config/opencode/oh-my-openagent.json(c)`.\n\n### D. PostHog telemetry (current state)\n- `src/shared/posthog.ts` (188 LOC): hardcoded API key `phc_CFJhj5HyvA62QPhvyaUCtaq23aUfznnijg5VaaGkNk74`, single event `omo_daily_active`, sha256(`PUBLISHED_PACKAGE_NAME + \":\" + hostname`) distinct_id.\n- `src/shared/posthog-activity-state.ts` (85 LOC): dedup via `~/.local/share/oh-my-opencode/posthog-activity.json` UTC-day check.\n- `src/shared/plugin-identity.ts`: PLUGIN_NAME=\"oh-my-openagent\", PUBLISHED_PACKAGE_NAME=\"oh-my-opencode\", CACHE_DIR_NAME=\"oh-my-opencode\".\n- Opt-out env vars: `OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0/false/no` or `OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1/true`.\n- Only call site in production: `src/cli/run/runner.ts` calls `trackActive(distinctId, \"run_started\")`. `createPluginPostHog()` exists but unused.\n\n### E. ROADMAP direction (`/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/ROADMAP.md`)\n- Multi-harness refactor in progress. Future Pi and Codex adapters MUST consume the Core packages layer.\n- \"We will NOT create a grand unified plugin interface that abstracts every harness.\"\n- Adapter shims should be thin wrappers around Core packages.\n\n## CONSTRAINTS\n\n1. **Behavior preservation**: existing `bunx oh-my-opencode install` and `bunx oh-my-openagent install` MUST continue to work unchanged. `--codex` is purely additive.\n2. **No path aliases** in src/ (e.g., `@/`). Use relative imports within a module, barrel imports across modules.\n3. **No emojis** in code or comments.\n4. **No `as any`, `@ts-ignore`, `@ts-expect-error`**.\n5. **No catch-all files** (`utils.ts`, `helpers.ts`, etc).\n6. **250 pure LOC ceiling** per file (rule: `.omo/rules/file-size-architectural-smell.md`).\n7. **TDD discipline**: regression test for new behavior FIRST, then implement.\n8. **Bun only**: never npm/yarn/pnpm at the root. But the vendored Codex components themselves use npm internally (we cannot rewrite them all in this PR).\n9. **Atomic commits**: separate concerns into discrete commits (vendor, installer, telemetry, docs).\n10. **PRs into dev MUST use `--merge` (merge commit), NOT squash/rebase.**\n\n## DESIGN CHOICES TO MAKE\n\nThe plan must decide and justify:\n\n1. **Where does the Codex plugin code live?** Options:\n   - (a) `packages/codex-plugin/` as a new workspace package (consistent with ROADMAP layering)\n   - (b) Top-level `codex-plugin/` directory (non-workspace)\n   - (c) Under `src/codex/` (adapter shim model)\n\n2. **How are the 5 components shipped?** Options:\n   - (a) Vendor source ONLY, build at npm-publish time via CI (smaller git, slower publish)\n   - (b) Vendor source AND prebuilt dist/ (larger git, faster install, matches existing codex-plugins pattern)\n   - (c) Rewrite components to consume omodex Core packages (out of scope for this PR per ROADMAP)\n\n3. **How does the `omo install --codex` flow install the Codex plugin?** Options:\n   - (a) Port `scripts/install-local.mjs` from Node to TypeScript inside `src/cli/install-codex/` and invoke from CLI\n   - (b) Embed `install-local.mjs` verbatim as a runtime script and shell out to it\n   - (c) Re-implement using existing omodex config-manager primitives\n\n4. **PostHog telemetry separation**:\n   - Same PostHog project + new event name `omo_codex_daily_active`?\n   - Or new project + new API key?\n   - Single global opt-out (`OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0`) or separate per-product opt-out (`OMO_CODEX_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1`)?\n\n5. **Bin entry strategy**: add `\"omo\": \"bin/oh-my-opencode.js\"` to root `package.json`. Should the JS shim ALSO be renamed/aliased? (Probably no - same shim, just one more bin name.)\n\n6. **TUI flow**: should `omo install` (no flags) show a single combined wizard asking about BOTH OpenCode providers AND Codex installation? Or should `--codex` be a separate pass?\n\n## DELIVERABLES (what the plan must produce)\n\nProduce a plan with:\n- A wave-based parallel execution graph (which tasks can run in parallel, which are sequential)\n- Per-task: WHERE (file paths), WHY (which scenario it advances), HOW (atomic steps), VERIFY (test/diagnostic command)\n- A scenario contract: at least 3 binary-observable scenarios that must PASS at completion, with their real-surface evidence (bunx invocation transcripts, `~/.codex/config.toml` diff, posthog event capture)\n- A list of files to CREATE vs EDIT (paths only, no full content)\n- Acceptance criteria mapped to scenarios\n- Explicit answers to the 6 design choices above\n- Identification of risks and blockers\n- A test-first ordering: which regression tests are written before which implementation\n\nThe plan will be handed off to a sub-agent for execution. Be exhaustive, leave nothing implicit."

For the first decision about where the Codex plugin code lives, I'm weighing three options: creating it as a new workspace package, placing it at the top level as a non-workspace directory, or nesting it under src/codex as an adapter shim. The ROADMAP explicitly states we won't create a unified plugin interface and that adapter shims should be thin wrappers, but the codex plugin components are currently vendored npm-based components with their own build systems rather than consuming omodex Core packages, which is planned as future work.

Option (a) as a workspace package would conflict with Bun's dependency management since the plugin uses npm internally. Option (b) at the top level breaks convention since top-level directories serve specific purposes. Option (c) under src/codex would inappropriately mix vendored npm code with the Bun-native source tree. I'm leaning toward a hybrid approach: placing it in `packages/codex-plugin/` as a directory but excluding it from the workspace array to keep it isolated while maintaining a logical structure.

The real constraint here is that the codex components have their own npm node_modules and build systems that need to be vendored. Since the ROADMAP envisions future adapters consuming Core packages but this PR is just porting existing code, I need a self-contained location for the vendored codex-plugin code with its own npm-based build system, separate from the Bun workspace. The installer code that handles this would sit in `src/cli/`.

For shipping the components, I'm weighing whether to vendor just source code and build at publish time versus vendoring prebuilt dist files. The existing installer already builds at install time, but since we're embedding this in the npm package itself, vendoring the prebuilt dist makes more sense—it lets `omo install --codex` just copy files without requiring npm to be available during installation.

The installer workflow would then copy the vendored components into the cache directory, run `npm install --omit=dev` for runtime dependencies, rewrite the paths in the config file, and update the main config. This approach keeps the user-facing install fast while still handling any runtime dependency setup needed.

Now I'm thinking through how to implement the `omo install --codex` command itself. The existing install script handles a lot—reading the marketplace manifest, building plugins, copying files, installing dependencies, rewriting paths, linking binaries, computing hashes, and pruning the cache. Rather than embedding it as a shell script or reimplementing everything from scratch, I should port it to TypeScript and integrate it directly into the CLI structure in `src/cli/install-codex/`. That way it fits naturally with the existing architecture and I can leverage the config-manager primitives where they apply.

For PostHog telemetry, keeping it in the same project makes sense since Codex is part of the OMO product family. I'll use a distinct event name like `omo_codex_daily_active` to separate Codex usage signals from OpenCode, and the distinct_id should track individual users across both products.

For opt-out behavior, the existing environment variables `OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0` or `OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1` should handle all telemetry globally—no need for per-product controls.

The bin entry strategy is straightforward: add `"omo"` to the root package.json pointing to the existing JS shim, which already detects the platform and spawns the correct binary. All three command names share this same entry point.

For the TUI flow, running `omo install` without flags shows the OpenCode wizard, while `omo install --codex` triggers the Codex-specific flow.  They write to different config locations and should remain separate, though you could run both in one command. But I need to reconsider the requirement—the existing `bunx oh-my-opencode install` and `bunx oh-my-openagent install` commands should also work through the new `omo` installer, which means the routing logic needs to handle all three entry points correctly. I'm realizing the `--codex` flag should work additively—when passed, it runs the Codex installer after the standard OpenCode flow completes, whether in TUI or non-TUI mode. For users who want Codex-only installation, I could use a combination like `--codex --skip-auth` to bypass the OpenCode provider prompts, or keep it simple and just make `--codex` always additive to the base install.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation: I need to create the vendored Codex plugin source tree under `packages/codex-plugin/`, then build out a new installer module at `src/cli/install-codex/` that handles the build, caching, and symlinking logic similar to the existing local installer.

Setting up the module structure... I need to reconsider the file structure with the 250 LOC limit in mind. I'm also thinking through how to handle the vendored code from codex-plugins, which has about 5 components that I'll need to compress and integrate.

For the vendoring strategy, I'm planning to copy the plugins/omo directory into packages/codex-plugin while preserving the component structure and prebuilt dist directories, then add a standalone package.json for that directory outside the workspace. At runtime, the installer will need to locate this vendored code within the npm package, copy it to the Codex cache directory, run a lean install, rewrite the MCP paths, link binaries, compute hook hashes, and update the config file.

Now I'm realizing the vendored code needs to be included in the npm publish via the root package.json's files array, so I need to add packages/codex-plugin/ to that list. The installer will then read from the installed package location and copy the contents to the Codex cache at runtime.

Finalizing the directory structure...

For PostHog telemetry, I want to keep it consistent with the existing CLI approach—same client creation and the single production call tracking daily active users with deduplication.

The question is whether to create a separate `omo_codex_daily_active` event or add a `source: "codex"` property to the existing event. The simplest approach is adding a `codex_installed: boolean` property to the current `omo_daily_active` event, which avoids duplicating the tracking logic.

Since the user wants Codex telemetry set up the same way OmO uses it with a disable toggle, I should track when the Codex plugin installs and potentially when it runs with the plugin active—though the plugin components themselves run inside Codex, so I need to think through where that instrumentation actually lives.

For this PR, install-time tracking is the right scope: emit an `omo_codex_install` event when `omo install --codex` completes, using the same PostHog API key, distinct_id derivation, and opt-out environment variables as OmO.

# Plan: Port codex-plugins into omodex, build `omo` installer with `--codex` flag

## Context

**User Request**: Port the local `codex-plugins` marketplace into `omodex`, add an `omo` CLI alias, a `--codex` install flag, PostHog telemetry matching the existing OmO pattern, and ensure backward compatibility of `bunx oh-my-opencode install` / `bunx oh-my-openagent install`.

**Codebase State**: omodex is a Bun-only OpenCode plugin with 11 agents, 54-61 hooks, Commander.js CLI, and an existing install wizard writing to `~/.config/opencode/` and `.opencode/`. codex-plugins is a separate npm-based repo with 5 isolated components (rules, comment-checker, lsp, ultrawork, ultragoal) and a Node.js installer writing to `~/.codex/`.

---

## Design Decisions (Justified)

### Decision 1: Where does the Codex plugin code live?

**Choice: `packages/codex-plugin/` as a new directory, NOT in the workspace array.**

Rationale:
- Consistent with ROADMAP layering (packages/ houses modules that will eventually become Core-consuming adapters).
- The components use npm + tsc + vitest internally -- they cannot participate in the Bun workspace yet.
- Keeping them out of the workspace array avoids Bun trying to resolve their npm deps.
- Cleaner than top-level `codex-plugin/` (packages/ is the established home for sub-packages) and cleaner than `src/codex/` (would pollute the Bun-native source tree).

### Decision 2: How are the 5 components shipped?

**Choice: (b) Vendor source AND prebuilt dist/.**

Rationale:
- The existing codex-plugins pattern ships prebuilt dist/. Including dist/ means `omo install --codex` can copy files to the cache without running tsc at install time.
- Exclude `.git/` and `node_modules/` from vendored copy (they add 111MB per component; source + dist is ~200KB per component).
- CI will rebuild dist/ before npm publish to ensure freshness.
- `npm install --omit=dev` is still needed at install time for runtime deps (picomatch, comment-checker binary, lsp-tools-mcp).
- The lsp-tools-mcp submodule gets flattened: copy its source/dist as regular files, not a git submodule reference.

### Decision 3: How does `omo install --codex` install the Codex plugin?

**Choice: (a) Port install-local.mjs to TypeScript in `src/cli/install-codex/`.**

Rationale:
- The existing installer is ~500 LOC of modern ESM across 7 files -- well-suited for a TypeScript port.
- TypeScript integrates naturally with the existing CLI architecture and gets compiled into the platform binary.
- Embedding the Node script (option b) is fragile. Re-implementing from scratch using config-manager (option c) is unnecessary since the config targets are fundamentally different (TOML vs JSONC, different directory structures).
- For TOML writing, use `smol-toml` (3KB, modern, Bun-compatible) -- it gets bundled at compile time.

### Decision 4: PostHog telemetry

**Choice: Same PostHog project, new event name `omo_codex_install`, single global opt-out.**

Rationale:
- Same project keeps analytics unified (same API key: `phc_CFJhj5HyvA62QPhvyaUCtaq23aUfznnijg5VaaGkNk74`).
- Event `omo_codex_install` fires once at `omo install --codex` completion (no daily dedup needed -- install is a one-time event).
- The same distinct_id derivation (SHA256 of `oh-my-opencode:hostname`) maintains user consistency.
- `OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0` and `OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1` disable ALL telemetry (both OpenCode and Codex). No per-product opt-out -- unnecessary complexity for the same product family.

### Decision 5: Bin entry strategy

**Choice: Add `"omo": "bin/oh-my-opencode.js"` to root `package.json`.**

Rationale:
- The existing JS shim handles platform detection and binary resolution. It doesn't care what name invoked it.
- All three names (`oh-my-opencode`, `oh-my-openagent`, `omo`) share the same shim and the same compiled binary.
- Zero additional code needed -- just one line in package.json.

### Decision 6: TUI flow

**Choice: `--codex` is a purely additive flag. Separate flow, not combined wizard.**

Rationale:
- `omo install` (no --codex) behaves identically to `bunx oh-my-opencode install` -- backward compat preserved.
- `omo install --codex` runs the normal OpenCode install AND THEN runs the Codex install step.
- The two install targets are fundamentally different: OpenCode writes JSONC to `~/.config/opencode/`, Codex writes TOML to `~/.codex/`. Combining them into one wizard would create confusing UX.
- In non-TUI mode: `omo install --codex --no-tui --claude yes ...` runs both installers sequentially.
- In TUI mode: `omo install --codex` shows the OpenCode wizard, then a separate Codex install spinner.

---

## Task Dependency Graph

| Task | Depends On | Reason |
|------|------------|--------|
| T1: Vendor codex-plugin source | None | Starting point, copies files from codex-plugins |
| T2: Add `omo` bin entry | None | One-line package.json edit, no deps |
| T3: Add `--codex` CLI types | None | Type additions only, no implementation |
| T4: Add smol-toml dependency | None | `bun add smol-toml` -- needed for TOML writing |
| T5: Write backward-compat regression tests | T3 | Needs types to exist, verifies existing install unaffected |
| T6: Write Codex installer unit tests | T1, T3, T4 | Needs vendored code structure + types + TOML dep |
| T7: Write Codex telemetry tests | T3 | Needs types, tests PostHog integration |
| T8: Implement Codex installer module | T6 | TDD: tests written first, then implementation |
| T9: Implement Codex telemetry | T7 | TDD: tests written first, then implementation |
| T10: Wire --codex into install routing | T8, T9 | Connects installer + telemetry to CLI flow |
| T11: Update package.json files array | T1 | Ensures vendored code ships in npm |
| T12: Update CI for codex-plugin | T8, T11 | CI must build codex dist before packaging |
| T13: End-to-end scenario verification | T10, T12 | Final gate: all 3 scenarios pass |

## Parallel Execution Graph

```
Wave 1 (Start immediately - no dependencies):
├── T1: Vendor codex-plugin source into packages/codex-plugin/
├── T2: Add "omo" bin entry to package.json
├── T3: Add --codex flag types (InstallArgs, Commander option)
└── T4: Add smol-toml dependency

Wave 2 (After Wave 1 completes - tests first):
├── T5: Write backward-compat regression tests
├── T6: Write Codex installer unit tests
└── T7: Write Codex telemetry tests

Wave 3 (After Wave 2 completes - implementation):
├── T8: Implement Codex installer module
└── T9: Implement Codex telemetry

Wave 4 (After Wave 3 completes - wiring):
├── T10: Wire --codex into install routing
└── T11: Update package.json files array

Wave 5 (After Wave 4 completes - CI & verification):
├── T12: Update CI for codex-plugin build
└── T13: End-to-end scenario verification

Critical Path: T1 → T6 → T8 → T10 → T13
Estimated Parallel Speedup: ~45% faster than sequential
```

## Tasks

### Task 1: Vendor codex-plugin source into packages/codex-plugin/

**Description**: Copy the omo plugin from `../codex-plugins/plugins/omo/` into `packages/codex-plugin/`, stripping `.git/` and `node_modules/` from each component. Flatten the lsp-tools-mcp submodule into regular files.

**Delegation Recommendation**:
- Category: `quick` - Straightforward file copy with filtering
- Skills: [] - No specialized skills needed, just file operations

**Skills Evaluation**:
- OMITTED `programming`: No code writing, just file copying
- OMITTED `git-master`: Not a git operation

**Depends On**: None
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `packages/codex-plugin/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` exists and is valid JSON
- `packages/codex-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` exists
- `packages/codex-plugin/.mcp.json` exists
- All 5 component directories exist under `packages/codex-plugin/components/`
- Each TS component has both `src/` and `dist/` directories
- `packages/codex-plugin/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py` exists
- `packages/codex-plugin/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js` exists (flattened, not submodule)
- No `.git/` or `node_modules/` directories anywhere in the vendored tree
- `packages/codex-plugin/` is NOT in root `package.json` workspaces array

**Files to CREATE**:
- `packages/codex-plugin/` (entire directory tree -- copied from `../codex-plugins/plugins/omo/`)
- `packages/codex-plugin/marketplace.json` (copied from `../codex-plugins/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`, path references updated)

**Atomic steps**:
1. Create `packages/codex-plugin/` directory
2. Copy `../codex-plugins/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/`, `hooks/`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, `README.md`
3. For each TS component (rules, comment-checker, lsp, ultragoal): copy `src/`, `dist/`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `README.md` -- skip `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `coverage/`
4. For ultrawork: copy entire directory minus `.git/`, `node_modules/`
5. For lsp: additionally copy `packages/lsp-tools-mcp/` from the component's submodule (initialized copy at `../codex-plugins/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/`), minus `.git/`
6. Copy marketplace.json, update the `source` path reference to point at `packages/codex-plugin/` relative to repo root
7. Verify no `.git` directories remain: `find packages/codex-plugin -name .git -type d`

**VERIFY**: `ls packages/codex-plugin/components/` shows 5 directories; `find packages/codex-plugin -name .git -type d` returns empty

### Task 8: Implement Codex installer module

**Description**: Port the install-local.mjs logic into TypeScript at `src/cli/install-codex/`. Each file must be under 250 LOC. Use `smol-toml` for TOML operations.

**Delegation Recommendation**:
- Category: `deep` - Complex multi-file TypeScript implementation porting from JavaScript
- Skills: [`programming`] - Strict TypeScript, 250 LOC ceiling, ESM, no any

**Skills Evaluation**:
- INCLUDED `programming`: TypeScript strict mode, no `as any`, 250 LOC ceiling, parse-don't-validate
- OMITTED `refactor`: Not refactoring existing code, writing new code

**Depends On**: T6 (tests must exist first -- TDD)
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- All unit tests from T6 pass
- Each file under 250 pure LOC
- No `as any`, `@ts-ignore`, or `@ts-expect-error`
- `bun run typecheck` passes
- Module exports `installCodexPlugin(options: CodexInstallOptions): Promise<CodexInstallResult>`

**Files to CREATE**:
- `src/cli/install-codex/index.ts` (barrel + orchestrator, <200 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-paths.ts` (path resolution, <80 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-manifest.ts` (marketplace + plugin manifest parsing, <100 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-cache.ts` (build, copy, prune, link operations, <200 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-mcp-rewriter.ts` (rewrite .mcp.json paths, <80 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-config-writer.ts` (TOML config mutation, <200 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/codex-hook-trust.ts` (SHA-256 fingerprinting, <100 LOC)
- `src/cli/install-codex/types.ts` (Codex-specific types, <80 LOC)

**Key implementation notes**:
- `codex-paths.ts`: Reads `CODEX_HOME` env (default `~/.codex`), `CODEX_LOCAL_BIN_DIR` env (default `~/.local/bin`). Resolves vendored plugin path relative to the installed package root using `import.meta.dir` or `import.meta.resolve`.
- `codex-cache.ts`: Uses `Bun.spawn` to run `npm install --omit=dev` in the cache directory. Uses `fs.cp` with `{ recursive: true, filter }` for atomic copy. Filter excludes `.git` and `node_modules`.
- `codex-config-writer.ts`: Uses `smol-toml` `parse()` and `stringify()`. Reads existing `~/.codex/config.toml`, merges sections, writes back. Creates file if absent.
- `codex-hook-trust.ts`: Computes SHA-256 of canonical JSON for each command hook. Same algorithm as `scripts/install/hook-trust.mjs`.
- `codex-mcp-rewriter.ts`: Reads `.mcp.json`, rewrites relative `args` paths to absolute pointing into cache dir.

**VERIFY**: `bun test src/cli/install-codex/` -- all tests pass

## Scenario Contracts

### Scenario 1: Backward Compatibility

**Command**: `bunx oh-my-opencode install --no-tui --claude yes --gemini no --copilot no --skip-auth`

**Observable Evidence**:
- Exit code 0
- `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json(c)` contains `oh-my-openagent@<version>` in plugins array
- `.opencode/oh-my-openagent.jsonc` is created/updated with Claude model config
- NO files written to `~/.codex/`
- NO `omo_codex_install` PostHog event emitted
- Console output does NOT mention "Codex"

### Scenario 2: Codex Install via `omo`

**Command**: `omo install --codex --no-tui --claude yes --gemini no --copilot no --skip-auth`

**Observable Evidence**:
- Exit code 0
- OpenCode configs written (same as Scenario 1)
- `~/.codex/plugins/cache/code-yeongyu-codex-plugins/omo/0.1.0/` exists with all 5 components
- `~/.codex/plugins/cache/.../components/rules/node_modules/picomatch/` exists (npm install ran)
- `~/.codex/config.toml` contains:
  ```toml
  [features]
  plugins = true
  plugin_hooks = true
  
  [plugins."omo@code-yeongyu-codex-plugins"]
  enabled = true
  
  [hooks.state."omo@code-yeongyu-codex-plugins:hooks/hooks.json:post_tool_use:0:0"]
  trusted_hash = "sha256:..."
  ```
- `~/.local/bin/codex-rules` symlink exists (or equivalent binaries)
- Console output includes "Codex plugin installed successfully"

### Scenario 3: Telemetry Opt-Out

**Command**: `OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1 omo install --codex --no-tui --claude yes --gemini no --copilot no --skip-auth`

**Observable Evidence**:
- Exit code 0
- Both OpenCode and Codex configs written normally
- PostHog client returns no-op (verified via test mock -- in production, no HTTP request to `us.i.posthog.com`)
- Console output still shows telemetry notice with opt-out instructions

## Commit Strategy (Atomic)

| # | Commit Message | Scope | Tasks |
|---|---------------|-------|-------|
| 1 | `feat: vendor codex-plugin source into packages/codex-plugin` | Vendoring only, no code changes | T1 |
| 2 | `feat: add omo bin entry and --codex CLI flag` | Types + package.json, no implementation | T2, T3, T4 |
| 3 | `test: add backward-compat regression tests for install flow` | Tests only | T5 |
| 4 | `test: add Codex installer and telemetry unit tests (red)` | Tests only (expected to fail) | T6, T7 |
| 5 | `feat: implement Codex installer module (src/cli/install-codex)` | Implementation, tests go green | T8 |
| 6 | `feat: implement Codex install telemetry` | Implementation, tests go green | T9 |
| 7 | `feat: wire --codex flag into install routing` | Integration wiring | T10, T11 |
| 8 | `ci: add codex-plugin build steps to CI and publish workflows` | CI/CD only | T12 |

---

## Risks & Blockers

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| `npm` not on PATH at install time | Codex install fails (can't install runtime deps) | Check for npm binary early, emit clear error: "npm is required for --codex. Install it from https://nodejs.org/" |
| lsp-tools-mcp submodule not initialized in codex-plugins | Vendoring misses lsp MCP dist | Build/init the submodule before vendoring: `cd ../codex-plugins/plugins/omo/components/lsp && npm run bootstrap` |
| smol-toml bundling with Bun | TOML library may not bundle cleanly | Test: `bun build src/cli/index.ts --target bun` with smol-toml imported. smol-toml is pure JS, should bundle fine. Fallback: use `@iarna/toml`. |
| Vendored dist/ staleness | Prebuilt dist may diverge from source | CI commit 8 ensures dist/ is rebuilt before packaging. Add a git hook or CI check: `npm run build && git diff --exit-code packages/codex-plugin/*/dist/` |
| Package size increase | ~1MB extra in npm tarball from vendored code | Acceptable. Current package is already ~5MB with platform binary stubs. 1MB is a 20% increase. |
| Codex config.toml format changes | Future Codex releases may change config format | Pin to known Codex config format. Log a version warning if `~/.codex/config.toml` has unknown sections. |
| Windows: symlink creation in ~/.local/bin | Windows doesn't support Unix symlinks by default | Skip binary linking on Windows. Codex CLI doesn't run on Windows currently. Add platform guard in codex-cache.ts. |

---

## Success Criteria

1. `bun test` passes (all existing + new tests)
2. `bun run typecheck` passes
3. `bun run build` succeeds
4. All 3 scenarios produce their expected observable evidence
5. `npm pack --dry-run` includes `packages/codex-plugin/` files
6. No file exceeds 250 pure LOC
7. PR passes CI on dev branch

- [ ] **T5. Write backward-compat regression tests**
  - What: Create `src/cli/install-codex-compat.test.ts` testing that install without --codex does NOT run any Codex operations. Mock config-manager functions, assert they are called unchanged. Test both `codex: undefined` and `codex: false`.
  - Depends: T3
  - Blocks: T10
  - Category: `deep`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: `bun test src/cli/install-codex-compat.test.ts` -- all tests pass

- [ ] **T6. Write Codex installer unit tests (TDD - red phase)**
  - What: Create 6 test files under `src/cli/install-codex/*.test.ts` covering all installer functions: paths, manifest, cache, config-writer, hook-trust, index orchestration. Tests should be complete and correct but will fail until T8 implements the module.
  - Depends: T1, T3, T4
  - Blocks: T8
  - Category: `deep`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: Test files exist and parse cleanly (`bun test src/cli/install-codex/ --bail 2>&1 | head -20` shows test discovery)

- [ ] **T7. Write Codex telemetry tests (TDD - red phase)**
  - What: Create `src/shared/codex-telemetry.test.ts` testing `trackCodexInstall()`: event emission, opt-out via both env vars, correct properties shape, distinct_id consistency.
  - Depends: T3
  - Blocks: T9
  - Category: `quick`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: Test file exists and parses cleanly

### Wave 3 (After Wave 2 Completes - Implementation)

- [ ] **T8. Implement Codex installer module**
  - What: Create 8 TypeScript files under `src/cli/install-codex/` porting the install-local.mjs logic. Each file under 250 LOC. Use smol-toml for TOML. Use Bun.spawn for npm. Use fs.cp with filter for cache copy. All T6 tests must go green.
  - Depends: T6
  - Blocks: T10
  - Category: `deep`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: `bun test src/cli/install-codex/` -- all tests pass; `bun run typecheck` passes

- [ ] **T9. Implement Codex telemetry**
  - What: Create `src/shared/codex-telemetry.ts` (<100 LOC) exporting `trackCodexInstall()`. Reuse `createCliPostHog()` and `getPostHogDistinctId()` from posthog.ts. Event: `omo_codex_install`. All T7 tests must go green.
  - Depends: T7
  - Blocks: T10
  - Category: `quick`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: `bun test src/shared/codex-telemetry.test.ts` -- all tests pass

### Wave 4 (After Wave 3 Completes - Wiring)

- [ ] **T10. Wire --codex into install routing**
  - What: Edit `src/cli/install.ts`, `src/cli/cli-installer.ts`, `src/cli/tui-installer.ts` to call `installCodexPlugin()` + `trackCodexInstall()` when `args.codex === true`, AFTER the OpenCode install step. Codex failure should not prevent OpenCode install from completing.
  - Depends: T8, T9
  - Blocks: T13
  - Category: `deep`
  - Skills: [`programming`]
  - QA: `bun test src/cli/` -- all tests pass including T5 regression

- [ ] **T11. Update package.json files array**
  - What: Add `"packages/codex-plugin/"` to the `files` array in root `package.json`
  - Depends: T1
  - Blocks: T12
  - Category: `quick`
  - Skills: []
  - QA: `npm pack --dry-run 2>&1 | grep codex-plugin` shows entries

### Wave 5 (After Wave 4 Completes - CI & Verification)

- [ ] **T12. Update CI for codex-plugin build**
  - What: Add build steps in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.github/workflows/publish.yml` to run `npm install && npm run build` in each TS component under `packages/codex-plugin/components/` before the main bun build step
  - Depends: T8, T11
  - Blocks: T13
  - Category: `quick`
  - Skills: []
  - QA: Workflow YAML is syntactically valid

- [ ] **T13. End-to-end scenario verification**
  - What: Run all 3 scenarios from the Scenario Contracts section above. Verify each observable evidence point. Document results.
  - Depends: T10, T12
  - Blocks: None (final gate)
  - Category: `deep`
  - Skills: [`programming`, `debugging`]
  - QA: All 3 scenarios pass; `bun test` passes; `bun run typecheck` passes; `bun run build` succeeds

## Execution Instructions

## Agent Catalog — 11 Built-in Agents

### Factory Pattern
All agents follow `createXXXAgent(model) -> AgentConfig` with a static `mode` property:
```typescript
const createXXXAgent: AgentFactory = (model: string) => ({ ... })
createXXXAgent.mode = "primary" | "subagent" | "all"
```
Excerpt from `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/agents/types.ts:24-26`:
```typescript
export type AgentFactory = ((model: string) => AgentConfig) & {
  mode: AgentMode;
};
```

### Registration Mechanism
Registration flows through **two layers**:

1. **Raw factories** in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/agents/builtin-agents.ts:32-45`:
```typescript
const agentSources: Record<BuiltinAgentName, AgentSource> = {
  sisyphus: createSisyphusAgent,
  hephaestus: createHephaestusAgent,
  oracle: createOracleAgent,
  librarian: createLibrarianAgent,
  explore: createExploreAgent,
  "multimodal-looker": createMultimodalLookerAgent,
  metis: createMetisAgent,
  momus: createMomusAgent,
  atlas: createAtlasAgent as AgentFactory,
  "sisyphus-junior": createSisyphusJuniorAgentWithOverrides as AgentFactory,
}
```

2. **Conditional wrappers** in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/agents/builtin-agents/` apply model resolution, overrides, and environment context.

3. **Final assembly** in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/plugin-handlers/agent-config-handler.ts:170-185` calls `createBuiltinAgents()`, then merges with user/project/plugin agents, applies protected-name filtering, and registers via `registerAgentName(name)` from `../features/claude-code-session-state`.

4. **Prometheus is special-cased** — no factory in `agentSources`. Built directly in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/plugin-handlers/prometheus-agent-config-builder.ts:40-127` via `buildPrometheusAgentConfig()`.

---

### Agent 1: Sisyphus (Main Orchestrator)

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Factory** | `createSisyphusAgent` in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/src/agents/sisyphus.ts:484` |

export function createSisyphusAgent(
  model: string,
  availableAgents?: AvailableAgent[],
  availableToolNames?: string[],
  availableSkills?: AvailableSkill[],
  availableCategories?: AvailableCategory[],
  useTaskSystem = false,
): AgentConfig {
  // ...
  return {
    description: "...",
    mode: MODE,
    model,
    maxTokens: 64000,
    prompt,
    color: "#00CED1",
    permission: {
      question: "allow",
      call_omo_agent: "deny",
      ...getFrontierToolSchemaPermission(model),
      ...getGptApplyPatchPermission(model),
    },
    reasoningEffort: "medium",
  };
}
```

<system-reminder>
Instructions from: /Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/packages/AGENTS.md
# packages/ — Monorepo Packages

**Generated:** 2026-05-20

## OVERVIEW

23 sibling packages across 4 roles. None of these are published as part of the main `oh-my-opencode` / `oh-my-openagent` npm dist (root `package.json` `files` only ships `dist/`, `bin/`, `postinstall.mjs`). They are sibling packages with their own publication / deployment targets.

## ROLE MAP

| Role | Count | Packages |
|------|-------|----------|
| **Platform binaries** | 11 | One per (OS × arch × variant). Uniform layout: `bin/` + `package.json` only. Selected at install time by `bin/` shim + `postinstall.mjs`. |
| **MCP packages** | 2 | `lsp-tools-mcp` (git submodule), `ast-grep-mcp` |
| **Core packages** | 9 | `utils`, `model-core`, `prompts-core`, `rules-engine` (was `rules-core`), `agents-md-core`, `ast-grep-core`, `comment-checker-core`, `hashline-core`, `boulder-state` |
| **Web** | 1 | `web` |

## PLATFORM BINARIES (11)

`oh-my-opencode-darwin-arm64`, `oh-my-opencode-darwin-x64`, `oh-my-opencode-darwin-x64-baseline`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-arm64`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-arm64-musl`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-x64`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-x64-baseline`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-x64-musl`, `oh-my-opencode-linux-x64-musl-baseline`, `oh-my-opencode-windows-x64`, `oh-my-opencode-windows-x64-baseline`.

Each contains only a `bin/<binary>` and a `package.json`. Built by [`script/build-binaries.ts`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/script/build-binaries.ts) via `bun compile`. Published by the `publish-platform.yml` workflow.

`-baseline` variants are pure x86_64 (no AVX2) for older CPUs. `-musl` variants link against musl libc for Alpine. Runtime selection happens in `bin/` and `postinstall.mjs`.

## MCP PACKAGES

| Package | Layout | Purpose |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `lsp-tools-mcp/` | Full standalone project (own `.git` submodule, `.github/`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `LICENSE`, `src/`, `test/`, `biome.json`, `vitest.config.ts`) | Serves `lsp_diagnostics`, `lsp_goto_definition`, `lsp_find_references`, `lsp_symbols`, `lsp_prepare_rename`, `lsp_rename`, `lsp_status` tools via stdio MCP. Registered as tier-1 MCP `lsp` in [`src/mcp/`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/src/mcp/). |
| `ast-grep-mcp/` | Internal package (`src/`, `dist/`, `tsconfig.json`) | Serves `ast_grep_search` + `ast_grep_replace` tools via stdio MCP. Registered as tier-1 MCP `ast_grep`. |

## CORE PACKAGES

| Package | Layout | Purpose |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `utils/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | Shared utilities: deep-merge, snake-case, frontmatter, file-utils, etc. |
| `model-core/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | Model resolution pipeline with ProviderCache dependency injection. |
| `prompts-core/` | `src/`, `prompts/`, `test/`, `tsconfig.json` | Harness-neutral markdown prompt loading, model-variant routing, and bundled mode prompts for search/analyze/team/hyperplan. |
| `rules-engine/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | Rule discovery + matching engine (renamed from `rules-core`). |
| `agents-md-core/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | AGENTS.md walk-up discovery and injection logic. |
| `ast-grep-core/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | ast-grep types, pattern-hints, and runner core with injectable spawn. |
| `comment-checker-core/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | apply-patch parser and binary runner with injectable spawn. |
| `hashline-core/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | Hashline edit primitives and diff helpers shared by adapter shims. |
| `boulder-state/` | `src/`, `tsconfig.json` | Work tracking state machine with split storage. |

## WEB

| Package | Sub-AGENTS.md | Purpose |
|---------|---------------|---------|
| `web/` | yes ([packages/web/AGENTS.md](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/packages/web/AGENTS.md)) | Marketing site. Next.js 15 + Cloudflare Workers via `@opennextjs/cloudflare`. Independent `bun.lock` + `tsconfig.json`. Only place in the repo where `@/*` path aliases are allowed. |

## CONVENTIONS

- **No new package without explicit need.** Adding a sibling package complicates publish + CI. Justify the boundary first.
- **Platform binaries** are generated. Do NOT edit by hand. Modify [`script/build-binaries.ts`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/script/build-binaries.ts).
- **`lsp-tools-mcp` is a git submodule.** Initialize with `git submodule update --init --recursive` after fresh clone.
- **`packages/web/` is excluded from root `bun test`** via `bunfig.toml`. It has its own [`web-ci.yml`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/.github/workflows/web-ci.yml) workflow.
- **CI builds** for non-platform packages run as part of the root `ci.yml`. Platform binaries build only via `publish-platform.yml` when triggered by `publish.yml`.

## ANTI-PATTERNS

- Never publish a sibling package manually. Use the GitHub Actions workflows.
- Never copy code between packages by hand. Either share via a core package or accept the duplication and document it.
- Never modify `bin/<binary>` inside a platform package — those are compiled artifacts.

</system-reminder>

[Project README: /Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/packages/omo-codex/plugin/README.md]
# omo

`omo` is the single local Codex plugin namespace for Yeongyu's Codex components.

Internally each component remains isolated under `components/`:

- `components/comment-checker`
- `components/rules`
- `components/lsp`
- `components/ultrawork`
- `components/ultragoal`

The root plugin manifest exports one Codex plugin named `omo`, with aggregate hooks, skills, and the LSP MCP server.

```

---

## Assistant (Sisyphus - Ultraworker · Opus 4.7 · 130.0s)

_Thinking:_

 I'm reviewing the AGENTS.md guidelines on package management. The key constraint is avoiding new packages without clear justification, since sibling packages complicate publishing and CI. For codex-plugin, the justification is solid—it's a separate harness adapter per the roadmap. I notice lsp-tools-mcp is already a submodule, but it's also embedded in the vendored codex-plugins under components/lsp/packages/, which creates duplication that should be cleaned up.

For now, I'll accept the ~5MB duplication since the embedded copy came through as regular files when I rsync'd without .git. In Phase 2, I can deduplicate by wiring the codex-lsp component to use the shared omodex submodule via symlink or relative path. For Phase 1, keeping it as-is is the pragmatic choice.

Now I'm setting up the omo-codex workspace structure with a package.json, strict TypeScript config, type barrel, and a src/ directory for new telemetry and install logic adapters.

Defining the directory layout... To ship the codex plugin to end users, we need to ship pre-built dist/ directories and the plugin/ folder within the npm package, so the CLI can copy them to ~/.codex/ without requiring npm install on the user's machine — all build complexity stays in CI.

The approach is to repurpose the existing install scripts: move the build logic into a CI script that runs during omodex build, then reimplement the cache and config logic in TypeScript within the CLI installer for a cleaner, fully type-safe user install step.

Defining the final package structure... I'm realizing I should split this into two parallel tracks: ULTRABRAIN handles the architectural contracts and specs (scenarios, API surface, idempotency guarantees, file-by-file changes), while DEEP agents tackle the actual implementation work in parallel. I have enough context to move forward, so let me start dispatching these tasks now rather than over-planning.

For DEEP-A specifically, I'm scoping out the package scaffolding and CLI integration work — setting up the new omo-codex package structure, wiring in the --codex flag, and updating the root package.json with workspaces and the omo bin entry. I'm breaking this down into manageable phases: first the package scaffold and workspace setup, then the CLI flag and install-codex TypeScript port, followed by telemetry integration, and finally documentation updates across multiple languages.

Since DEEP-A1 touches the root package.json and DEEP-A2 does too, I need to run A1 first to avoid file conflicts, then parallelize A2 and B, and save docs for after both land.

I'll handle the package scaffolding myself since I know the conventions well enough to move fast.

Writing package.json... I'm realizing the index.d.ts can't export from a non-existent file, so I'll create a minimal src/index.ts stub instead and keep the type barrel simple. My plan is to scaffold the package structure myself with package.json, tsconfig, and minimal entry points, then dispatch the deep agents in parallel to handle CLI, telemetry, and docs work separately.

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/packages/omo-codex/README.md",
  "content": "# @oh-my-opencode/omo-codex\n\nCodex harness adapter for **oh-my-openagent**. Brings the OMO experience (rules injection, comment checker, LSP MCP, ultrawork, ultragoal) into [OpenAI Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex) through Codex's native plugin system.\n\n## Layout\n\n| Path | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `plugin/` | Vendored Codex plugin namespace `omo` with 5 components. Shipped to the user via `~/.codex/plugins/cache/`. |\n| `marketplace.json` | Codex marketplace manifest. Identifies `omo` as the single installable plugin. |\n| `scripts/` | Node ESM build scripts (port of the original `codex-plugins/scripts/install-local.mjs`). |\n| `src/` | TypeScript runtime: installer + telemetry consumed by the omodex CLI. |\n| `MARKETPLACE.md` | Vendored upstream marketplace README. |\n\n## Components Vendored\n\n- `rules` (TypeScript) - injects `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / `.omo/rules/**` into context via `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `PostToolUse`, `PostCompact`.\n- `comment-checker` (TypeScript) - runs `@code-yeongyu/comment-checker` after `apply_patch` / `edit` / `write` tool use.\n- `lsp` (TypeScript + LSP MCP) - exposes LSP diagnostics, navigation, symbols, rename via MCP + post-edit hooks.\n- `ultrawork` (Python) - keyword detector (`ulw` / `ultrawork`) that injects the full ultrawork directive, plus a `SessionStart` hook that syncs bundled agent TOML files into `CODEX_HOME/agents`.\n- `ultragoal` (TypeScript) - durable multi-goal orchestration backed by `.omo/ultragoal/` evidence audit.\n\n## Install\n\nEnd users invoke through the omodex CLI:\n\n```bash\nbunx omo install --codex=yes\n# or, equivalently:\nbunx oh-my-opencode install --codex=yes\nbunx oh-my-openagent install --codex=yes\n```\n\nThe installer copies the built plugin into `~/.codex/plugins/cache/code-yeongyu-codex-plugins/omo/<version>/`, enables it in `~/.codex/config.toml`, and links the per-component binaries into `~/.local/bin/` (or `CODEX_LOCAL_BIN_DIR`).\n\n## Telemetry\n\nAnonymous telemetry uses the same PostHog project as oh-my-openagent but emits the distinct event `omo_codex_daily_active`. Opt out with:\n\n```bash\nexport OMO_CODEX_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1\n# or globally for every OMO product:\nexport OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1\n```\n\nSee `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/docs/legal/privacy-policy.md` for the full disclosure.\n\n## Provenance\n\nVendored from [`code-yeongyu/codex-plugins`](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-plugins) at the snapshot present in `/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/codex-plugins/` on 2026-05-25. Per-component upstream:\n\n- [code-yeongyu/codex-rules](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-rules)\n- [code-yeongyu/codex-comment-checker](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-comment-checker)\n- [code-yeongyu/codex-lsp](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-lsp)\n- [code-yeongyu/codex-ultrawork](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-ultrawork)\n- [code-yeongyu/codex-ultragoal](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/codex-ultragoal)\n"
}
```

## Assistant (Sisyphus - Ultraworker · Opus 4.7 · 258.2s)

_Thinking:_

 The dependencies installed successfully. Rather than getting bogged down in extra verification steps, I'll do a quick sanity check and then kick off the DEEP-A and DEEP-B tasks in parallel as background work, saving the commit for the end.

Actually, thinking about the commit strategy — I should commit the vendor and scaffold changes now for a clean history, then dispatch the deep agents afterward. But first, let me reconsider what ULTRABRAIN should tackle here. The tricky architectural pieces are around idempotency (detecting if codex is already installed, handling version upgrades, skipping redundant rebuilds), but these are pretty standard design problems that DEEP agents can handle fine. But I need to be careful about file conflicts if multiple agents edit package.json at once. I should probably commit the current state first, then dispatch, to avoid merge conflicts. The only potential collision point is package.json, so I need to be explicit with DEEP-A to only add build scripts and dependencies, not modify existing fields, and skip any build:omo-codex-plugin additions since the infrastructure is already in place. The TypeScript port needs to use bun-friendly imports, reference the vendored plugin at the correct path using import.meta.dir, read marketplace.json from the omodex packages directory, and be idempotent so it's safe to run multiple times. After installation completes, I'll print a success message and optionally call the telemetry module if available.

I need to preserve all existing install flows for --claude/--gemini/--copilot, avoid modifying the vendored plugin or scripts directories, stick to node built-ins and existing dependencies, and keep each file under 250 lines of pure code. The plugin name is "oh-my-openagent" from the shared plugin identity, and after install the plugin should be enabled in the Codex home directory at $CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex, with the local bin at $CODEX_LOCAL_BIN_DIR or ~/.local/bin. I need to set up the telemetry module for omo-codex by first examining the existing PostHog implementation in the shared utilities as a reference, then creating a parallel telemetry structure in the omo-codex package that mirrors the pattern but with its own environment variable controls while also respecting the global flags. Writing test cases...

The PostHog API key needs to match omodex's so events end up in the same project, with the platform and product_name properties distinguishing them. I need to avoid importing from the omodex shared directory entirely—telemetry stays self-contained in omo-codex. I also can't modify any omodex files, skip type assertions, or exceed 250 lines of pure code per file. The workspace has posthog-node and @oh-my-opencode/utils available as dependencies.

The existing omodex telemetry only runs from the CLI runner, and while a plugin-side factory exists, it's unused. For omo-codex, I should expose both factories without wiring them into the install flow yet—DEEP-A will handle that integration. I need to verify everything with test runs, type checking, and a successful build, then report back on files created, tests added, and any blockers.

I'm launching both DEEP-A and DEEP-B as background tasks, but deferring DEEP-D docs until the concrete code is ready. The DEEP-A task is substantial (500–800 lines across multiple files) and DEEP-B is smaller (200–300 lines), both within deep agent scope. One concern: after Bun builds, the src directory structure differs from dist, which could affect the import.meta.dir pattern DEEP-A mentioned—I'll need to watch for that.

The tricky part is resolving the vendored plugin path at runtime. The bundled CLI ends up at dist/cli/index.js, but the plugin lives at packages/omo-codex/plugin/. Since Bun bundles everything into one file, the plugin directory needs to be accessible as a sibling. I could use a hardcoded relative path from the npm package root, but dev-time paths differ from post-build paths. The cleanest approach is to walk up the directory tree until finding the package.json with name "oh-my-opencode", which works regardless of whether we're running from src or dist. I'll let the deep agent handle this detail.

I also need to make sure the root package.json's `files` field includes `packages/omo-codex/plugin` so it gets published with the npm package—I'll handle that myself during integration rather than delegating it.

`getPostHogDistinctId(): string` returns `sha256(\"omo-codex:\" + os.hostname()).hex()` — DIFFERENT from omodex (which hashes \"oh-my-opencode:...\"), so installations of both products can be counted as distinct\n     - <path>/Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omodex/CHANGELOG.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Changelog
2: 
3: All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
4: 
5: The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
6: and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7: 
8: ## [Unreleased] (next-minor or 4.3.0)
9: 
10: ### Added
11: 
12: - `default_mode` config auto-activates ultrawork and ralph loop without typing commands. Set it once in your plugin config and every new session starts in high-agency mode. (PR #4190)
13: - Toast i18n with English and Chinese locales, backed by plugin config. UI messages now respect your language setting. (PR #3884)
14: - `disabled_providers` config schema and helper. Block providers you do not use from appearing in model resolution and fallback chains. (PR #4031)
15: - `plan-format-validator` hook warns when task labels in `.omo/plans/*.md` are malformed, catching plan syntax errors before execution. (PR #4221)
16: - Prometheus gains spec-driven development framework awareness (OpenSpec, .specify). The planner now reads specification files and factors them into interview questions and task breakdowns. (PR #2307)
17: - Per-agent skill filtering. Skills can declare `restrictedAgents` so only eligible agents see them in prompts and tool descriptions. (PR #2827)
18: - `look_at` async refactor. Image analysis dispatches non-blocking and returns when ready, keeping the session responsive during multimodal work. (PR #4098)
19: - `keyword-detector.enabled_expansions` config gives allowlist control over which keyword expansions fire. (PR #4084)
20: - `doctor` CLI now warns when `oh-my-openagent/tui` is missing from `tui.json`, catching incomplete installs. (PR #4048)
21: - `taskCleanupDelayMs` configurable for background tasks. Tune how long finished background task artifacts linger before cleanup. (PR #3241)
22: - Per-agent `displayName` for i18n. Agents can present localized names in UI and logs. (PR #4081)
23: - Grok family models registered with `reasoningEffort` support. (PR #4186)
24: - CLI `setup` alias for `install`. Either command runs the interactive setup wizard. (PR #4174)
25: 
26: ### Changed
27: 
28: - Massive package layering refactor. Eight workspace packages extracted: `utils`, `hashline-core`, `model-core`, `rules-engine` (renamed from `rules-core`), `agents-md-core`, `ast-grep-core`, `comment-checker-core`, and `boulder-state`. This is the foundation for the multi-harness roadmap.
29: - `model-core` now uses dependency injection, eliminating all `src/` back-imports from core packages. The host harness injects its snapshot fetcher, suggestion parser, and context-limit resolver.
30: - `prompt-async-gate` split from a 779-line monolith into six focused sub-modules: reservations, queue, message state, dispatch runner, and a thin facade.

(Showing lines 1-30 of 176. Use offset=31 to continue.)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Privacy Policy
2: 
3: Last updated: May 2, 2026
4: 
5: This Privacy Policy explains how oh-my-opencode and oh-my-openagent collect, use, and protect information related to the published CLI package, the OpenCode plugin, and the project website or repository materials where they apply.
6: 
7: For this policy, "Application" means the published `oh-my-opencode` CLI package and the OpenCode plugin runtime it installs. "Service" means the Application and the project distribution surfaces together. "We" and "our" refer to the maintainer of oh-my-opencode. "You" refers to a user of the Service.
8: 
9: By using the Service, you accept this Privacy Policy and the accompanying Terms of Service in [terms-of-service.md](./terms-of-service.md).
10: 
11: ## 1. Information We Collect
12: 
13: We collect limited non-personal information needed to operate and improve the Service.
14: 
15: ### Automatically collected information
16: 
17: When anonymous telemetry is enabled, the Application may collect a single anonymous usage event:
18: 
19: - `omo_daily_active`, sent at most once per UTC day per machine when the plugin loads or when the `run` CLI is invoked, used to estimate daily, weekly, and monthly active installations
20: - Anonymous machine metadata bundled with that event, such as package version, plugin name, runtime, OS family, locale, and timezone
21: - A pseudonymous installation identifier derived from a one-way hash of the local hostname
22: 
23: The Application does not create or update PostHog person profiles, and does not collect prompt contents, source files, repository contents, access tokens, API keys, raw hostnames, or runtime error diagnostics through this telemetry path.
24: 
25: ### Configuration and local state
26: 
27: The Application stores local configuration and telemetry deduplication state on your machine to support installation, configuration, and anonymous daily active tracking.
28: 
29: ## 2. How Telemetry Works
30: 
31: The Application uses PostHog for anonymous product analytics. Telemetry is enabled by default, following the same opt-out posture used in cmux, and is intended only to estimate active installations (daily, weekly, and monthly) so we can understand broad adoption.
32: 
33: Telemetry can be disabled at any time by setting one of these environment variables before running the CLI or plugin host:
34: 
35: ```bash
36: export OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0
37: # or
38: export OMO_DISABLE_POSTHOG=1
39: ```
40: 
41: When telemetry is disabled, PostHog events are not sent.
42: 
43: ## 3. Third-Party Services
44: 
45: The Service may use third-party providers including:
46: 
47: - **PostHog** for anonymous product analytics
48: - **npm** and **GitHub** for package distribution, releases, and repository hosting
49: - **OpenCode** and model providers that you configure separately for your own agent usage
50: 
51: Each third-party service has its own terms and privacy practices.
52: 
53: ## 4. How We Use Information
54: 
55: We use collected information to:
56: 
57: - Estimate daily, weekly, and monthly active installations
58: - Understand aggregate adoption across operating systems and package versions
59: - Maintain and evolve the Service
60: 
61: We do not sell personal information collected through this telemetry path.
62: 
63: ## 5. Data Retention
64: 
65: Anonymous analytics are retained only as long as reasonably necessary for understanding adoption. Local telemetry state stored on your machine remains there until removed by you.
66: 
67: ## 6. Your Choices
68: 
69: You may:
70: 
71: - Disable anonymous telemetry through environment variables
72: - Remove local configuration or cached state files from your machine
73: - Stop using the Service at any time
74: 
75: ## 7. Security
76: 
77: We use reasonable administrative and technical measures to protect the systems we control. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure.
78: 
79: ## 8. Changes to This Policy
80: 
81: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material updates will be reflected by revising the date at the top of this document.
82: 
83: ## 9. Contact
84: 
85: Questions about this Privacy Policy should be raised through the project repository issue tracker or the maintainer contact channels published in the repository.

(End of file - total 85 lines)
</content>

## STRUCTURE

```
docs/
├── manifesto.md                              # The "why" — referenced from README
├── model-capabilities-maintenance.md         # How model-capabilities cache is refreshed
├── guide/                                    # User-facing tutorial-style guides (5 files)
├── reference/                                # API / config / CLI reference (7 files)
├── examples/                                 # Sample JSONC configs (3 files)
├── legal/                                    # privacy-policy.md + terms-of-service.md
├── superpowers/
│   ├── plans/                                # In-flight design plans (model-settings, background-task-retry, log-rotation)
│   └── specs/                                # Frozen design specs (model-settings-compatibility, background-task-retry-timeline)
└── troubleshooting/
    └── ollama.md
```

## CONVENTIONS

- **User-facing language only in `guide/` and `reference/`.** No `OmO` internal jargon without explanation.
- **`superpowers/` is internal.** Design docs, plans, RFCs. Outside readers should not be expected to follow these.
- **Path links** use the `file://` scheme so OpenCode renders them in TUI. Use absolute paths.
- **No HTML.** Markdown only. No `<details>` / `<summary>` (causes rendering issues in some terminals).
- **Code blocks** use language fences. Use `jsonc` for config snippets to preserve comments.
- **Docs touching `packages/web/` re-trigger the web CI** via [`web-ci.yml`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/.github/workflows/web-ci.yml).

## ANTI-PATTERNS

- Never add a doc to `guide/` or `reference/` without a `WHERE TO LOOK` entry above.
- Never paste agent-facing system prompts here. Those live in [`src/agents/`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/src/agents/) or [`src/features/builtin-skills/`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/src/features/builtin-skills/).
- Never document changing config keys without also updating [`src/config/schema/`](file:///Users/yeongyu/local-workspaces/omo/src/config/schema/) and re-running `bun run build:schema`.

</system-reminder>
```

Skip if `dist/` exists and source hash unchanged? Always rebuild? Specify the exact check.\n\nG. **The `npm install` size**: each component has heavy devDeps (vitest, biome, etc.). ## A. Where component `dist/` artifacts get built

**Decision:** Choose A1 as the target strategy: CI/publish builds `packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/*/dist`, and npm ships prebuilt artifacts.

**Rationale:** Codex requires `components/<name>/dist/cli.js` at runtime, and users expect `bunx omo install --codex=yes` to be fast. A2 pulls heavy devDeps and needs network. A3 keeps installs simple but makes git diffs large and risks stale generated code.

**Concrete artifact:**

Required publish-time artifacts:

```text
packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js
packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/rules/dist/cli.js
packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/lsp/dist/cli.js
packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js
packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/ultragoal/dist/cli.js
```

`components/ultrawork` has no `dist`; it remains Python hook source.

---

## B. Package `files` and scripts for A1

**Decision:** Add the Codex plugin assets to root `files`, add `build:omo-codex`, and make root `build` run it. `prepublishOnly` should call it through `bun run build`.

**Rationale:** Root remains Bun + ESM, but Codex plugin build stays isolated behind `npm --prefix packages/omo-codex/plugin`. Shipping `packages/omo-codex/plugin` is required because Codex cache install copies that directory. `prepublishOnly` must cover manual `npm publish`, not only GitHub Actions.

**Concrete artifact:**

Root `package.json` additions:

```diff
 "files": [
   "dist",
   "bin",
   "postinstall.mjs",
   "packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist",
-  "packages/ast-grep-mcp/dist"
+  "packages/ast-grep-mcp/dist",
+  "packages/omo-codex/marketplace.json",
+  "packages/omo-codex/plugin",
+  "packages/omo-codex/scripts/install-local.mjs",
+  "packages/omo-codex/scripts/install"
 ],
 "scripts": {
-  "build": "bun run build:ast-grep-mcp && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target bun --format esm --external @ast-grep/napi --external zod && bun run build:node-require-shim && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly && bun build src/cli/index.ts --outdir dist/cli --target bun --format esm --external @ast-grep/napi && bun run build:schema",
+  "build": "bun run build:ast-grep-mcp && bun run build:omo-codex && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target bun --format esm --external @ast-grep/napi --external zod && bun run build:node-require-shim && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly && bun build src/cli/index.ts --outdir dist/cli --target bun --format esm --external @ast-grep/napi && bun run build:schema",
+  "build:omo-codex": "npm --prefix packages/omo-codex/plugin ci && npm --prefix packages/omo-codex/plugin run build && bun run verify:omo-codex",
+  "verify:omo-codex": "test -f packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js && test -f packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/rules/dist/cli.js && test -f packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/lsp/dist/cli.js && test -f packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js && test -f packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/ultragoal/dist/cli.js",
   "prepublishOnly": "bun run clean && bun run build:lsp-tools-mcp && bun run build"
 }
```

Prerequisite: add and commit `packages/omo-codex/plugin/package-lock.json` generated with npm `11.12.1`. If the first PR intentionally avoids that lockfile, use `npm --prefix packages/omo-codex/plugin install` only as the temporary A2 bridge.

Plugin `package.json` should make build order explicit:

```diff
- "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
+ "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/lsp-tools-mcp && npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-comment-checker && npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-rules && npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-lsp && npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-ultragoal",
```

---

## C. Publish workflow changes for A1

**Decision:** Keep `publish-main` on Node 24, but pin npm to `11.12.1`; root `bun run build` then builds Codex artifacts. Platform binary workflow needs no change.

**Rationale:** The main npm package is the only artifact that ships `packages/omo-codex/plugin`. Optional platform packages do not contain the Codex plugin. Pinning npm matches the vendored component `packageManager` fields and avoids npm latest drift.

**Concrete artifact:**

In `.github/workflows/publish.yml`, replace the npm upgrade step:

```diff
- - name: Upgrade npm for trusted publishing (>=11.5.1)
-   run: npm install -g npm@latest
+ - name: Install pinned npm for Codex plugin build and trusted publishing
+   run: npm install -g npm@11.12.1
```

Add npm cache to existing `actions/setup-node@v6`:

```diff
 - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
   with:
     node-version: "24"
     registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
+    cache: "npm"
+    cache-dependency-path: |
+      packages/omo-codex/plugin/package-lock.json
```

Add a post-build verification step:

```yaml
- name: Verify Codex plugin artifacts
  if: steps.check.outputs.skip != 'true'
  run: bun run verify:omo-codex
```

No `.github/workflows/publish-platform.yml` change.

---

## D. A2 user-install build flow

**Decision:** A2 is only a first-PR fallback. If used, `omo install --codex=yes` builds with `npm`, not `node`, before copying or enabling Codex config.

**Rationale:** The upstream installer already follows `npm install` then `npm run build`, then copies to cache and runs `npm install --omit=dev`. That gives a working source-only install without CI changes, but it is slower and not fully offline-safe.

**Concrete artifact:**

Exact spawn sequence:

```ts
spawn("npm", ["--prefix", pluginRoot, "install"], {
  stdio: "inherit",
  timeout: 120_000,
})

spawn("npm", ["--prefix", pluginRoot, "run", "build"], {
  stdio: "inherit",
  timeout: 120_000,
})

spawn("npm", ["--prefix", cachePluginRoot, "install", "--omit=dev", "--no-audit", "--no-fund"], {
  stdio: "inherit",
  timeout: 60_000,
})
```

Failure mode for missing npm:

```text
Codex plugin install needs npm because this package does not contain prebuilt Codex artifacts.
Install Node.js/npm, or upgrade to an omo release that ships prebuilt Codex plugin dist.
No Codex plugin was enabled.
```

Skip rebuild when all required `dist/cli.js` files exist and `.omo-codex-build.json.sourceHash` matches the current source hash.

---

## E. Component build interdependency

**Decision:** Build `components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp` before `components/lsp`. Do not rely on current workspace order.

**Rationale:** `codex-lsp` has a file dependency on its embedded `lsp-tools-mcp`, and runtime `.mcp.json` invokes the nested MCP `dist/cli.js` directly. The `codex-lsp` `prebuild` script can bootstrap it, but deterministic release builds should make this order explicit.

**Concrete artifact:**

Build order from `packages/omo-codex/plugin`:

```bash
npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/lsp-tools-mcp
npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-comment-checker
npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-rules
npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-lsp
npm run build --workspace @code-yeongyu/codex-ultragoal
```

`codex-lsp` may keep its `prebuild` bootstrap as a local-development safety net, but CI should already have `lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js`.

---

## F. Idempotency

**Decision:** Skip runtime build/install when the cache has the same plugin version, required artifacts exist, and a source-hash manifest matches. Do not always rebuild.

**Rationale:** Second `omo install --codex=yes` must be fast. `dist` existence alone is too weak because source can change without deleting `dist`. Version alone is too weak for local development installs.

**Concrete artifact:**

Write this after a successful build:

```json
{
  "schema": 1,
  "npm": "11.12.1",
  "sourceHash": "sha256:<hex>",
  "artifacts": [
    "components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js",
    "components/rules/dist/cli.js",
    "components/lsp/dist/cli.js",
    "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js",
    "components/ultragoal/dist/cli.js"
  ]
}
```

Hash inputs:

```text
plugin/package.json
plugin/package-lock.json
plugin/.codex-plugin/plugin.json
plugin/.mcp.json
plugin/hooks/hooks.json
plugin/scripts/**
plugin/components/**/package.json
plugin/components/**/tsconfig*.json
plugin/components/**/src/**
plugin/components/**/hooks/**
plugin/components/**/skills/**
plugin/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/**
```

Exclude:

```text
node_modules
dist
coverage
.git
```

---

## G. Runtime `npm install --omit=dev`

**Decision:** Confirm the upstream pattern: after copying to Codex cache, run runtime-only install with `--omit=dev`.

**Rationale:** Build-time devDeps must never be installed in the user's Codex cache. Runtime dependencies still need to resolve from the cache path, especially `rules` dependency `picomatch`, `comment-checker` optional dependency, and `codex-lsp` file dependency wiring.

**Concrete artifact:**

```bash
npm --prefix "${cachePluginRoot}" install --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund
```

Do not run bare `npm install` in the cache. Under A1, this is the only npm operation allowed at user install time, and it can be skipped only if runtime dependency probing proves the copied plugin is self-contained.

---

## H. First PR recommendation

**Decision:** For the first PR, choose A2 as a temporary minimum viable path if CI workflow changes are out of scope.

**Rationale:** It gets a working install from source-only vendored components using the existing upstream build-then-install pattern. It avoids blocking the PR on publish workflow edits. It does not satisfy the final fast/offline target, so it must be marked as a bridge to A1.

**Concrete artifact:**

First PR behavior:

```text
1. Ship packages/omo-codex/plugin in root package.json files.
2. On omo install --codex=yes, check required dist artifacts.
3. If missing or sourceHash changed, run:
   npm --prefix <package>/packages/omo-codex/plugin install
   npm --prefix <package>/packages/omo-codex/plugin run build
4. Copy plugin to ~/.codex/plugins/cache/code-yeongyu-codex-plugins/omo/<version>.
5. Run npm --prefix <cachePluginRoot> install --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund.
6. Enable Codex config only after all artifacts and runtime deps are present.
```

User-facing warning on first source build:

```text
Building Codex plugin artifacts locally. This one-time step may take up to 2 minutes.
Future releases will ship prebuilt Codex artifacts.
```

---

## I. Long-term migration path

**Decision:** As Codex components migrate to omodex Core packages, keep Codex as a thin Node adapter and move shared logic builds into the existing root package pipeline.

**Rationale:** ROADMAP says Core must be harness-neutral, with adapters depending downward only. Codex should stop carrying duplicated rules/comment/LSP logic once `@oh-my-opencode/*` Core packages are stable for adapter consumption.

**Concrete artifact:**

1. Replace duplicated component logic with imports from Core packages: `@oh-my-opencode/rules-engine`, `@oh-my-opencode/agents-md-core`, `@oh-my-opencode/comment-checker-core`, and later shared LSP package strategy.
2. Build Core packages first with root Bun/tsgo pipeline, then build Codex adapter with isolated npm/tsc.
3. Remove heavy per-component devDeps where components become thin wrappers; keep Vitest/Biome only where adapter-specific tests remain.
4. Collapse embedded `components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp` into a single source of truth, either root `packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist` copied into plugin or a published runtime dependency.
5. Add Codex hook smoke tests to CI after build:
   ```bash
   node packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/rules/dist/cli.js hook session-start < fixture.json
   node packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js hook post-tool-use < fixture.json
   node packages/omo-codex/plugin/components/lsp/dist/cli.js hook post-tool-use < fixture.json
   ```

---

## FIRST-PR CHECKLIST

1. Add `packages/omo-codex/plugin` and Codex install assets to root `package.json.files`.
2. Fix `packages/omo-codex/marketplace.json` source path or installer resolution so it points at the vendored `plugin/` directory.
3. Implement `omo install --codex=yes` to build from source only when required `dist/cli.js` artifacts are missing.
4. Use exact A2 spawn commands with `npm --prefix <pluginRoot> install` and `npm --prefix <pluginRoot> run build`.
5. After copying to Codex cache, run `npm --prefix <cachePluginRoot> install --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund`.
6. Add `.omo-codex-build.json` source-hash idempotency so the second install skips rebuild.
7. Verify the cache contains all required `dist/cli.js` files before enabling Codex config.
8. Do not edit publish workflows in the first PR unless choosing to implement A1 immediately.
9. Add a follow-up issue or TODO for A1: pinned npm 11.12.1, plugin lockfile, CI prebuild, publish artifact verification.

## OPEN QUESTIONS FOR USER

1. Is a temporary first-PR source build that can exceed 30 seconds acceptable if it is explicitly labeled as a bridge to A1?
2. Should the A1 release also bundle runtime dependencies into `dist` for fully offline install, or is `npm install --omit=dev` with npm cache fallback acceptable for "offline-friendly"?

14: export type PostHogActivityReason = "install_started" | "install_completed" | "cli_run"
15: export type PostHogClient = {
16:   readonly trackActive: (distinctId: string, reason: PostHogActivityReason) => void
17:   readonly shutdown: () => Promise<void>
18: }
19:

21:   trackActive: () => undefined,
22:   shutdown: async () => undefined,
23: }
24: 
25: let activityStateProviderOverride: typeof getPostHogActivityCaptureState | null = null
26: type OsProvider = Pick<typeof os, "arch" | "cpus" | "hostname" | "platform" | "release" | "totalmem" | "type">

31: }
